root.text: Hmm? A living one. Curious. Few cross that gate these days.

who.text: Morrik. Court wizard, once. The dungeon kept my mind and my hat; the rest it took as payment. I study the old magics buried beneath this tavern now.

place.text: The Rusty Flagon — the last warm hearth before the deep dungeon. Grum keeps the ale honest. Mind the merchant's prices, though.

root.text: Welcome to the Rusty Flagon! Ale's fresh, beds are mostly clean, and the skeleton tips poorly but behaves.

choices.label: Got a room for the night?

room.text: Five gold a night, breakfast included — assuming the crows didn't get at the bread again. Beds are upstairs... once I build the stairs.

news.text: They say the west corridor runs deeper than it should. Took three torches down there myself and the dark still wouldn't budge. Keep the gate shut, will you?

root.text: ♪ ...oh the dungeon's deep and the ale is cheap... ♪ — Care for a verse, traveler?

song.text: ♪ The mage in the corner once had hair, now the candlelight shines right through him there; he pays in dust and he drinks in vain, but he always says please all the same. ♪

song2.text: ♪ Grum built a door to keep out the night, the night walked in and asked for a light... ♪ — That one's still missing a last verse. Come back when I've lived it.

rumor.text: The merchant over there pays good coin for anything shiny you haul out of the dark. Don't ask where it ends up — he certainly doesn't.

root.text: Psst. Potions, torches, trinkets — finest goods this side of the gate, and no questions either way.

choices.label: Where does this stuff come from?

source.text: Adventurers go down. Their gear comes up. Simple economics, friend — somebody has to complete the circle.
